articles.title: Iraq Is Keeping Its Syria Oil Route—Even If Hormuz Reopens
articles.summary: Nobody, especially not Iraq, wants to be caught relying on Hormuz ever again. Iraq is preparing to export crude oil and naphtha through Syria's Mediterranean port of Baniyas, expanding an emergency workaround that emerged after the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupted the country's primary export routes and left storage tanks filling up. According to Reuters, Iraqi officials say the strategy will remain in place even after shipping through Hormuz returns to normal. That alone says plenty. Iraq normally exports around 3.6 million barrels of…

articles.title: Quote of the day by Clarence Budington Kelland on Father's Day: 'My father didn't tell me how to live; he lived, and….'
articles.summary: Clarence Budington Kelland's message emphasizes that a father's actions are the greatest teaching tool. Children observe and learn values through their father's example rather than through spoken advice.

articles.title: The first primates may have evolved in the cold, not the tropics
articles.summary: A surprising new study suggests the earliest primates didn't originate in tropical forests but in cold, dry parts of North America. Some may have even survived seasonal Arctic conditions by slowing their metabolism or hibernating. Researchers found that dramatic climate shifts, rather than warmth, played a major role in driving primate evolution and expansion. The discovery reshapes our understanding of how our own lineage began.

articles.title: The U.S. States Leading the Backlash Against Data Centers

Data centres are expected to be a major consumer of U.S. power by 2030 and beyond, as several tech companies expand their network, which is growing consumer concerns around the country’s power supply and demand. This has led several states to halt or even ban data centre development. Globally, data centres are expected to consume twice as much power and water by 2030, according…

articles.title: US-Iran Delegations Expected in Switzerland
articles.summary: Three days after President Trump signed an agreement with Iran, Switzerland is playing host for diplomatic talks with the United States, Iran, Pakistan, and Qatar in attendance. The Associated Press's International Correspondent Philip Crowther joins Bloomberg This Weekend with hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini to discuss who may be attending this summit and what a recent strikes in Lebanon could mean for any deal. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: The Latest in College Pricing: Tuition at 10 Percent of Your Income
articles.summary: Whitman College is trying to keep it simple: Tell us your adjusted gross income, and we won’t make you apply and get in just to receive a price quote.

articles.title: Quote of the day on Father's Day 2026: ‘The most important thing a father can do for his children is..’
articles.summary: Father's Day 2026 special quote by Theodore Hesburgh talk about the most crucial quality of being a good father. It talks about the mother-father bond and how it can impact a child.

articles.title: Equity mutual funds fell upto 6% last week. Check top 5 laggards
articles.summary: Equity mutual funds experienced a downturn last week, with some correcting up to 6%. International funds were particularly hard hit, with the DSP World Mining Overseas Equity Omni FoF leading the decline at nearly 5.81%.

articles.title: Born on 21 June? You share your birthday with Lana Del Rey, Prince William, Benazir Bhutto
articles.summary: If you are celebrating your birthday on 21 June, then here's a list of renowned personalities, including Lana Del Rey, Prince William, Benazir Bhutto and others who were born on this date as well. Check it out.

articles.title: Cottage Cheese Shortages Are Driven by TikTok and ‘Protein-Maxxing’
articles.summary: TikTok and “protein-maxxing” are driving a cottage cheese squeeze that’s left some consumers desperate.

articles.title: History Repeating Itself in America
articles.summary: As the nation prepares to celebrate its 250th anniversary, we can look back and see the recurring themes and lessons in American history. Host of the podcast History That Doesn't Suck and professor at Utah Valley University's Center for Constitutional Studies Greg Jackson joins Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss his new book Been There, Done That, How Our History Shows What We Can Overcome with hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini, which explores these moments in history. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
articles.summary: The move comes after weeks of anti-government protests that have caused a shortage of basic goods in Bolivia.
